title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Inherited Deafness
summary:
Big news in medicine! The FDA has approved the first gene therapy for a type of inherited deafness. Gene therapy is a treatment that fixes a problem in your genes (the instructions inside your cells).

The therapy is called Otarmeni. It treats hearing loss caused by a problem in a gene called OTOF. This gene makes a protein called otoferlin, which helps your ear send sound signals to your brain. If the gene is broken, the signals can't get through, and the person is born deaf.

Otarmeni uses a harmless virus to deliver a working copy of the OTOF gene into the ear. In a trial with 20 people, 16 showed improved hearing within six months. One more improved within a year. Some could even hear whispers! All the people who got better could hear well enough that they didn't need a cochlear implant (a device that helps deaf people hear).

The company that makes Otarmeni, Regeneron, says the treatment itself will be free for patients in the U.S. (but you might still have to pay for the doctor visit). This is a huge step forward for gene therapy and for people with hearing loss.
why_it_matters: This breakthrough could help kids who are born deaf hear for the first time, showing how science can change lives and give people new abilities.

keywords.explanation: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the agency that decides if new medicines are safe to use.
A treatment that fixes a broken gene inside your cells to cure a disease.
Deafness that a person is born with because of a problem in their genes passed from parents.
A protein that helps the ear send sound signals to the brain.
A small electronic device that helps deaf people hear by sending signals to the ear.
The company that makes the gene therapy called Otarmeni.

to protect the ear from loud noises

questions.question: Who will pay for the treatment itself in the U.S.?

background_read: Gene therapy is a new kind of medicine that changes a person's genes to fix diseases at the source.
The FDA (the Food and Drug Administration, the U.S. agency that approves medicines) decides if treatments are safe enough for people.
Deafness can be caused by many things, and this therapy only works for a specific type caused by a broken OTOF gene.
Article_Structure: WHO: The FDA, the company Regeneron, and patients with inherited deafness.
WHAT: The FDA approved the first gene therapy for a type of inherited deafness.
WHERE: In the United States, where the treatment is approved.
WHY: To help people born with a broken OTOF gene hear better without needing a cochlear implant.

perspectives.description: They are very hopeful because this therapy could let deaf children hear without needing a special device.
They see this as a huge step forward for gene therapy, proving it can work for more than rare diseases.
Some people worry about side effects or note that the doctor visit might still cost money, even if the treatment is free.
